title: My Personal Anxiety Toolkit
description: Your go-to anxiety toolkit and top 5 tools — fill this in and download it to keep handy for when you need it most.
instructions: Out of everything you have learned, some tools will resonate more deeply with you than others. Fill in your personal toolkit below — your crisis responses and your top 5 go-to tools. Knowing these by heart makes it easy to reach for them when anxiety rises.

items.label: When anxiety rises, my quick physical intervention is:
items.placeholder: e.g., Box breathing (4-4-4-4), cold water on wrists, TIPP technique

items.label: My go-to grounding technique is:
items.placeholder: e.g., 5-4-3-2-1 senses exercise, feet on the ground, cold object in hand

items.label: My coping thought is:
items.placeholder: e.g., 'This feeling is temporary and I have handled it before'

items.label: My support person I can call or text:
items.placeholder: e.g., My partner Sarah (555-0123), My friend James, My therapist Dr. Lee

items.label: My #1 tool from this course:
items.placeholder: e.g., TIPP Skill for crisis moments

items.label: My #3 tool:
items.placeholder: e.g., Riding the Wave when emotions are intense

items.label: My #4 tool:
items.placeholder: e.g., Fear Ladder for gradual exposure

disclaimer: This card is a personal coping tool. If you are in crisis, contact the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline by calling or texting 988, or text HOME to 741741 for the Crisis Text Line.
